channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Magnus Carlsen set to ditch Grand Chess Tour for $1.5M Esports World Cup
item.content:encoded:
Magnus Carlsen and other major names in competitive chess are set to skip the Grand Chess Tour in favour of the Esports World Cup.

Fellow chess grandmaster Hikaru Nakamura confirmed on his stream that he, Carlsen, and Alireza Firouzja will all miss the GCT series of events to compete in Riyadh.

“As far as I know, neither EWC nor GCT have changed the dates. I’m going to be playing EWC, Nakamura revealed.

“Alireza Firouzja is going to be playing EWC. He made the choice of playing EWC over the Grand Chess Tour. Magnus, of course, will be playing the EWC.”

Carlsen won the first-ever Chess tournament at the Esports World Cup last year, taking home $250k of the tournament’s substantial prize pool.

As the reigning champion, Carlsen automatically qualifies for this year’s event and will represent Team Liquid again.

Meanwhile, last year’s runner-up Firouzja has also qualified for EWC 2026 through the Speed Chess Championship and will represent Team Falcons.

Grand Chess Tour and Esports World Cup Clash

Taking place across four locations, the Grand Chess Tour will run from May 3 all the way to August 28.

This will likely lead to a clash with the lucrative Esports World Cup, which takes place between July and August.

Specifically, the $1.5 million Chess tournament at the Esports World Cup is scheduled to take place from August 11 to August 15.

And with the prize pool of this year’s EWC chess tournament expected to be $1.5m again, it’s no surpirse that players are prioritising it.

Meanwhile, the Grand Chess Tour 2026’s base prize pool is $100k, though there will also be additional prize money available in the finals, Rapid, and Blitz tournaments.

What do you think about chess being included in esports tournaments? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

It can’t be too different to the autobattler TeamFight Tactics at the Esports World Cup 2026.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PlayStation Plus May 2026 Monthly Games Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Players have been informed that they will receive free monthly games in May 2026 through PlayStation Plus Essential.

The time has arrived for players to check out the new free games for the month, and, as we know, games are revealed by tier. We already got some really good picks in April, like Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered, Crew Motorfest, and more. May doesn’t seem like it will disappoint either, as it comes with one of the most popular sports games ever.

EA Sports FC 26 and 2 More Games Available Through PlayStation Plus in May

Announced through a new blog post, the next set of PlayStation Plus free monthly games will arrive on May 5, and you will need a PS Plus Essential membership to claim the titles. The list includes the latest installment of one of the most popular sports video game series of all time, EA Sports FC 26 (PS5/PS4). Additionally, players receive the new soulslike action RPG Wuchang: Fallen Feathers (PS5) and the hand-drawn action-platformer Nine Sols (PS5/PS4).

What’s more, PS Plus members will also be able to claim the EA Sports FC 26 PlayStation Plus Icons Pack this month. This is a special add-on that can be redeemed while the game is available through the collection. Those are just the games in the first membership tier. Later on in the month, we will be informed about the PlayStation Plus Game Catalog and Classics Catalog titles for May 2026, which will be available for PS Plus Extra and Premium members.

In other news, 7 games will be removed from the PlayStation Plus library in May. Additionally, PlayStation 5 has received a new April 2026 update, and here are all the changes and tweaks in the patch notes. What are your thoughts on the PlayStation Plus May 2026 monthly games? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Troy Baker beginning conversations about creating his own game. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
